title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: alkahest lightweight, dependency-free toolbox pre-processing XY data experimental methods (.e. signal can measured along continuous variable). provides methods baseline estimation correction, smoothing, normalization, integration peaks detection.

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: can install released version alkahest CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("alkahest") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/alkahest")

title: Pre-Processing XY Data from Experimental Methods
text: alkahest expects input data simplest form (two-column matrix data frame, two-element list two numeric vectors).
code: ## Load the package library(alkahest) ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Whittaker smoothing smooth <- smooth_whittaker(XRD, lambda = 1000, d = 2, sparse = TRUE)  ## 4S Peak Filling baseline baseline <- baseline_peakfilling(smooth, n = 10, m = 5, by = 10)  plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(baseline, type = "l", col = "red") ## Correct baseline XRD$count <- XRD$count - baseline$y  ## Find peaks peaks <- peaks_find(XRD, SNR = 3, m = 5)  plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(peaks, type = "p", pch = 16, col = "red") ## Simulate data set.seed(12345) x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) z <- y + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01) # Add some noise  ## Plot raw data plot(x, z, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "", main = "Raw data") lines(x, y, type = "l", lty = 2, col = "red")  ## Savitzky–Golay filter smooth <- smooth_savitzky(x, z, m = 21, p = 2) plot(smooth, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "", main = "Savitzky–Golay filter") lines(x, y, type = "l", lty = 2, col = "red")

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
text: Linear Baseline Estimation

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
code: baseline_linear(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric baseline_linear(x, y, points = range(x))  # S4 method for ANY,missing baseline_linear(x, points = range(x))

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. points numeric vector specifying data points used fitting process (x unit).

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Linear Baseline Estimation — baseline_linear
code: ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Plot spectrum plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")  ## Linear baseline baseline <- baseline_linear(XRD, points = c(25, 34))  plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(baseline, type = "l", col = "red")   ## Correct baseline XRD$count <- XRD$count - baseline$y  plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
text: Baseline estimation iterative mean suppression.

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
code: baseline_peakfilling(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric baseline_peakfilling(x, y, n, m, by = 10)  # S4 method for ANY,missing baseline_peakfilling(x, n, m, by = 10)

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. n integer value giving number iterations. m odd integer giving half window size. integer specifying binning ratio (.e. number points grouped together).

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
text: Liland, K. H. (2015). 4S Peak Filling - baseline estimation iterative mean suppression. MethodsX, 2, 135-140. doi:10.1016/j.mex.2015.02.009 .

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
text: N. Frerebeau

title: 4S Peak Filling — baseline_peakfilling
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")  ## 4S Peak Filling baseline <- baseline_peakfilling(BEGe, n = 5, m = 5, by = 2)  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(baseline, type = "l", col = "red")

title: SNIP Baseline Estimation — baseline_snip
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. LLS logical scalar: LLS operator applied x employing SNIP algorithm? used method "SNIP". decreasing logical scalar: decreasing clipping window used? n integer value giving number iterations.

title: SNIP Baseline Estimation — baseline_snip
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: SNIP Baseline Estimation — baseline_snip
text: Morháč, M., Kliman, J., Matoušek, V., Veselský, M. & Turzo, . (1997). Background elimination methods multidimensional gamma-ray spectra. Nuclear Instruments Methods Physics Research Section : Accelerators, Spectrometers, Detectors Associated Equipment, 401(1), p. 113-132. doi:10.1016/S0168-9002(97)01023-1 Morháč, M. & Matoušek, V. (2008). Peak Clipping Algorithms Background Estimation Spectroscopic Data. Applied Spectroscopy, 62(1), p. 91-106. doi:10.1366/000370208783412762 Ryan, C. G., Clayton, E., Griffin, W. L., Sie, S. H. & Cousens, D. R. (1988). SNIP, statistics-sensitive background treatment quantitative analysis PIXE spectra geoscience applications. Nuclear Instruments Methods Physics Research Section B: Beam Interactions Materials Atoms, 34(3), p. 396-402. doi:10.1016/0168-583X(88)90063-8

text: N. Frerebeau

title: Rectangle Rule — integrate_rectangle
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. right logical scalar: right rule used instead left rule?

title: Rectangle Rule — integrate_rectangle

title: Rectangle Rule — integrate_rectangle
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Rectangle Rule — integrate_rectangle
code: ## Calculate the area under the sine curve from 0 to pi # integrate(f = function(x) x^3, lower = 0, upper = 2) x <- seq(0, 2, len = 101) y <- x^3  plot(x, y, type = "l")   integrate_rectangle(x, y, right = FALSE) # 3.9204 #> [1] 3.9204 integrate_rectangle(x, y, right = TRUE) # 4.0804 #> [1] 4.0804 integrate_trapezoid(x, y) # 4.0004 #> [1] 4.0004

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: Finds local maxima sequential data.

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
code: peaks_find(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric peaks_find(x, y, method = c("MAD"), SNR = 2, m = NULL, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing peaks_find(x, method = c("MAD"), SNR = 2, m = NULL, ...)

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra parameters passed internal methods. method character string specifying method used background noise estimation (see ). SNR integer giving signal--noise-ratio peak detection (see ). m odd integer giving half window size. NULL, 5% data points used half window size.

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: local maximum highest one given window higher \(SNR \times noise\) recognized peak. following methods available noise estimation: MAD Median Absolute Deviation.

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: Adapted Stasia Grinberg's findPeaks function.

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Find Peaks — peaks_find
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Correct baseline BEGe_correct <- signal_correct(BEGe, method = "SNIP")  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_correct, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") abline(h = mad(BEGe_correct$y) * 3) # noise threshold  ## Find peaks BEGe_peaks <- peaks_find(BEGe_correct, SNR = 3, m = 5) lines(BEGe_peaks, type = "p", pch = 16, col = "red")   ## Half-Width at Half-Maximum x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 1000) y <- dnorm(x)  peaks_fwhm(x, y, center = 0) # Expected: 2 * sqrt(2 * log(2)) #> [1] 2.354354

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
text: Estimates Half-Width Half-Maximum (FWHM) given peak.

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
code: peaks_fwhm(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric peaks_fwhm(x, y, center)  # S4 method for ANY,missing peaks_fwhm(x, center)

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. center numeric value giving peak position x units.

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
text: numeric value.

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
text: tries get smallest possible estimate.

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Half-Width at Half-Maximum — peaks_fwhm
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Correct baseline BEGe_correct <- signal_correct(BEGe, method = "SNIP")  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_correct, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") abline(h = mad(BEGe_correct$y) * 3) # noise threshold  ## Find peaks BEGe_peaks <- peaks_find(BEGe_correct, SNR = 3, m = 5) lines(BEGe_peaks, type = "p", pch = 16, col = "red")   ## Half-Width at Half-Maximum x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 1000) y <- dnorm(x)  peaks_fwhm(x, y, center = 0) # Expected: 2 * sqrt(2 * log(2)) #> [1] 2.354354

title: Bin — resample_bin
text: Averages x values applies function corresponding y values.

title: Bin — resample_bin
code: resample_bin(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric resample_bin(x, y, by, f = mean, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing resample_bin(x, y, by, f = sum)

title: Bin — resample_bin
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra parameters passed f. integer specifying binning ratio (.e. number points grouped together). f function takes numeric vector intensities argument returns single numeric vector. Used estimate local representative value bin (defaults sum(); see examples).

title: Bin — resample_bin
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Bin — resample_bin
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Bin — resample_bin
code: ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Plot spectrum plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")   ## Bin by 3 XRD_bin_mean <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = mean) XRD_bin_min <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = min) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(25, 35), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_bin_mean, type = "l", col = "red") lines(XRD_bin_min, type = "l", col = "green")   ## Downsample by 10 XRD_down <- resample_down(XRD, by = 10) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_down, type = "l", col = "red")   ## Linearly interpolate XRD_approx <- resample_interpolate(XRD, from = 20, to = 40, by = 0.02) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_approx, type = "l", col = "red")

title: Downsample — resample_down
text: Downsample

title: Downsample — resample_down
code: ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Plot spectrum plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")   ## Bin by 3 XRD_bin_mean <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = mean) XRD_bin_min <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = min) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(25, 35), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_bin_mean, type = "l", col = "red") lines(XRD_bin_min, type = "l", col = "green")   ## Downsample by 10 XRD_down <- resample_down(XRD, by = 10) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_down, type = "l", col = "red")   ## Linearly interpolate XRD_approx <- resample_interpolate(XRD, from = 20, to = 40, by = 0.02) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_approx, type = "l", col = "red")

title: Linearly Interpolate — resample_interpolate
code: ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Plot spectrum plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")   ## Bin by 3 XRD_bin_mean <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = mean) XRD_bin_min <- resample_bin(XRD, by = 3, f = min) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(25, 35), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_bin_mean, type = "l", col = "red") lines(XRD_bin_min, type = "l", col = "green")   ## Downsample by 10 XRD_down <- resample_down(XRD, by = 10) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_down, type = "l", col = "red")   ## Linearly interpolate XRD_approx <- resample_interpolate(XRD, from = 20, to = 40, by = 0.02) plot(XRD, type = "l", xlim = c(20, 40), xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count") lines(XRD_approx, type = "l", col = "red")

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
text: Rescales intensities area curve (AUC) equal 1.

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
code: rescale_area(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_area(x, y, method = c("rectangle", "trapezoid"), ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_area(x, method = c("rectangle", "trapezoid"), ...)

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. method character string specifying method integration. must one "rectangle" "trapezoid". unambiguous substring can given.

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Normalize intensities by AUC — rescale_area
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Normalize by area under the curve BEGe_area <- rescale_area(BEGe) plot(BEGe_area, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")  integrate_rectangle(BEGe) #> [1] 147922.1 integrate_rectangle(BEGe_area) #> [1] 1  ## Rescale so that intensities sum to 1 BEGe_total <- rescale_total(BEGe, total = 1) plot(BEGe_total, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rescale intensities to 0-1 BEGe_range <- rescale_range(BEGe, min = 0, max = 1) plot(BEGe_range, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

what: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
text: Rescales intensities specified minimum maximum.

title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
code: rescale_range(x, y, ...)  rescale_min(x, y, ...)  rescale_max(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_range(x, y, min = 0, max = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_range(x, min = 0, max = 1)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_min(x, y, min = 0)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_min(x, min = 0)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_max(x, y, max = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_max(x, max = 1)

title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. min legnth-one numeric vector specifying output minimum. max legnth-one numeric vector specifying output maximum.

title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Rescales intensities to have specified minimum and maximum — rescale_range
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Normalize by area under the curve BEGe_area <- rescale_area(BEGe) plot(BEGe_area, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")  integrate_rectangle(BEGe) #> [1] 147922.1 integrate_rectangle(BEGe_area) #> [1] 1  ## Rescale so that intensities sum to 1 BEGe_total <- rescale_total(BEGe, total = 1) plot(BEGe_total, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rescale intensities to 0-1 BEGe_range <- rescale_range(BEGe, min = 0, max = 1) plot(BEGe_range, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

what: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
text: Rescales intensities sum specified value.

title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
code: rescale_total(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_total(x, y, total = 1)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_total(x, total = 1)

title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. total legnth-one numeric vector specifying output total. Defaults 1, .e. normalizes total intensity.

title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Rescales intensities to sum to a specified value — rescale_total
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Normalize by area under the curve BEGe_area <- rescale_area(BEGe) plot(BEGe_area, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")  integrate_rectangle(BEGe) #> [1] 147922.1 integrate_rectangle(BEGe_area) #> [1] 1  ## Rescale so that intensities sum to 1 BEGe_total <- rescale_total(BEGe, total = 1) plot(BEGe_total, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Rescale intensities to 0-1 BEGe_range <- rescale_range(BEGe, min = 0, max = 1) plot(BEGe_range, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
text: Transform Intensities

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
code: rescale_transform(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric rescale_transform(x, y, f, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing rescale_transform(x, f, ...)

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra arguments passed f. f function takes numeric vector intensities argument returns numeric vector.

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
text: Transformation intensities can used improve identification peaks low signal--noise ratio.

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Transform Intensities — rescale_transform
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Transform intensities BEGe_trans <- rescale_transform(BEGe, f = sqrt) plot(BEGe_trans, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "sqrt(Count)")

title: Baseline Correction — signal_correct
text: Baseline Correction

title: Baseline Correction — signal_correct
code: signal_correct(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_correct(x, y, method = c("linear", "rubberband", "SNIP", "4S"), ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_correct(x, method = c("linear", "rubberband", "SNIP", "4S"), ...)

title: Baseline Correction — signal_correct
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra arguments passed baseline_*() (see details). method character string specifying method baseline estimation. must one "linear", "rubberband", "SNIP" "4S" (see details). unambiguous substring can given.

title: Baseline Correction — signal_correct
text: Available methods baseline estimation: linear Linear baseline estimation (see baseline_linear()). rubberband Rubberband baseline estimation (see baseline_rubberband()). SNIP Sensitive Nonlinear Iterative Peak clipping algorithm (see baseline_snip()). 4S 4S Peak Filling (see baseline_peakfilling()).

title: Baseline Correction — signal_correct
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Subset from 2.75 to 200 keV BEGe <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Baseline correction BEGe_corr <- signal_correct(BEGe, method = "SNIP")  plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count") lines(BEGe_corr, type = "l", col = "red")

what: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift

text: Shift X Scale Given Value

title: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift
code: signal_shift(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_shift(x, y, lag)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_shift(x, y, lag)

title: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. lag length-one numeric vector specifying offset.

title: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Shift the X Scale by a Given Value — signal_shift
code: ## X-ray diffraction data("XRD")  ## Plot spectrum plot(XRD, type = "l", xlab = expression(2*theta), ylab = "Count")  ## Shift by one degree XRD_offset <- signal_shift(XRD, lag = 1) lines(XRD_offset, type = "l", col = "red")

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
text: Smoothes intensities loess fitting.

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
code: smooth_loess(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75, ...)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_loess(x, span = 0.75, ...)

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Extra arguments passed stats::loess(). span integer specifying degree smoothing (see stats::loess()).

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Loess Smoothing — smooth_loess
code: ## Simulate data with some noise x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01)  ## Plot spectrum plot(x, y, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Rectangular smoothing unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3) plot(unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Triangular smoothing weighted <- smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 5) plot(weighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Loess smoothing loess <- smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75) plot(loess, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 21, p = 2) plot(savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Whittaker smoothing whittaker <- smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2) plot(whittaker, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
text: Unweighted sliding-average rectangular Smoothing.

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
code: smooth_rectangular(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_rectangular(x, m)

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. m odd integer giving number adjacent points used.

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
text: replaces point signal average \(m\) adjacent points. \((m - 1) / 2\) points beginning end spectrum complete \(m\)-width smooth calculated. prevent data loss, progressively smaller smooths used ends spectrum.

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Rectangular Smoothing — smooth_rectangular
code: ## Simulate data with some noise x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01)  ## Plot spectrum plot(x, y, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Rectangular smoothing unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3) plot(unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Triangular smoothing weighted <- smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 5) plot(weighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Loess smoothing loess <- smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75) plot(loess, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 21, p = 2) plot(savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Whittaker smoothing whittaker <- smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2) plot(whittaker, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: Savitzky-Golay Filter

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
code: smooth_savitzky(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 3, p = 2)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_savitzky(x, m, p)

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. m odd integer giving number adjacent points used. p integer giving degree polynomial used.

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: method based least-squares fitting polynomials segments \(m\) adjacent points. \((m - 1) / 2\) points beginning end spectrum complete \(m\)-width smooth calculated. prevent data loss, original \((m - 1) / 2\) points ends spectrum preserved.

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: Gorry, P. . (1990). General Least-Squares Smoothing Differentiation Convolution (Savitzky-Golay) Method. Analytical Chemistry, 62(6), p. 570-573. doi:10.1021/ac00205a007 . Savitzky, . & Golay, M. J. E. (1964). Smoothing Differentiation Data Simplified Least Squares Procedures. Analytical Chemistry, 36(8), p. 1627-1639. doi:10.1021/ac60214a047 .

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Savitzky-Golay Filter — smooth_savitzky
code: ## Simulate data with some noise x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01)  ## Plot spectrum plot(x, y, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Rectangular smoothing unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3) plot(unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Triangular smoothing weighted <- smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 5) plot(weighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Loess smoothing loess <- smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75) plot(loess, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 21, p = 2) plot(savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Whittaker smoothing whittaker <- smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2) plot(whittaker, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")

title: Triangular Smoothing — smooth_triangular
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. m odd integer giving number adjacent points used.

text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Triangular Smoothing — smooth_triangular
text: replaces point signal weighted mean \(m\) adjacent points. \((m - 1) / 2\) points beginning end spectrum complete \(m\)-width smooth calculated. prevent data loss, progressively smaller smooths used ends spectrum.

title: Triangular Smoothing — smooth_triangular
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Triangular Smoothing — smooth_triangular
code: ## Simulate data with some noise x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01)  ## Plot spectrum plot(x, y, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Rectangular smoothing unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3) plot(unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Triangular smoothing weighted <- smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 5) plot(weighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Loess smoothing loess <- smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75) plot(loess, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 21, p = 2) plot(savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Whittaker smoothing whittaker <- smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2) plot(whittaker, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")

title: Whittaker Smoothing — smooth_whittaker
code: smooth_whittaker(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2, sparse = FALSE)  # S4 method for ANY,missing smooth_whittaker(x, lambda = 1600, d = 2, sparse = FALSE)

title: Whittaker Smoothing — smooth_whittaker
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. lambda integer giving smoothing parameter. larger lambda , smoother curve. d integer specifying order penalty. sparse logical scalar: sparse matrices used computation? TRUE, Matrix required.

title: Whittaker Smoothing — smooth_whittaker
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Whittaker Smoothing — smooth_whittaker
text: Eilers, P. H. C. (2003). Perfect Smoother. Analytical Chemistry, 75(14): 3631-36. doi:10.1021/ac034173t .

text: N. Frerebeau

title: Whittaker Smoothing — smooth_whittaker
code: ## Simulate data with some noise x <- seq(-4, 4, length = 100) y <- dnorm(x) + rnorm(100, mean = 0, sd = 0.01)  ## Plot spectrum plot(x, y, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Rectangular smoothing unweighted <- smooth_rectangular(x, y, m = 3) plot(unweighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Triangular smoothing weighted <- smooth_triangular(x, y, m = 5) plot(weighted, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Loess smoothing loess <- smooth_loess(x, y, span = 0.75) plot(loess, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Savitzky–Golay filter savitzky <- smooth_savitzky(x, y, m = 21, p = 2) plot(savitzky, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")   ## Whittaker smoothing whittaker <- smooth_whittaker(x, y, lambda = 1600, d = 2) plot(whittaker, type = "l", xlab = "", ylab = "")

title: Subset — subset
text: signal_select() allows subset values x. signal_slice() allows subset position along x.

title: Subset — subset
code: signal_select(x, y, ...)  signal_slice(x, y, ...)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_select(x, y, from, to)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_select(x, from, to)  # S4 method for numeric,numeric signal_slice(x, y, subset)  # S4 method for ANY,missing signal_slice(x, subset)

title: Subset — subset
text: x, y numeric vector. y missing, attempt made interpret x suitable way (see grDevices::xy.coords()). ... Currently used. , numeric value giving first last value (x unit) selected. subset integer vector giving either positive values keep, negative values drop. values provided must either positive negative (coerced integer .integer()).

title: Subset — subset
text: Returns list two components x y.

title: Subset — subset
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Subset — subset
code: ## gamma-ray spectrometry data("BEGe")  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Subset from 2.75 keV to 200 keV BEGe_1 <- signal_select(BEGe, from = 3, to = 200)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_1, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")   ## Subset from the 20th to the 1250th value BEGe_2 <- signal_slice(BEGe, subset = 20:1250)  ## Plot spectrum plot(BEGe_2, type = "l", xlab = "Energy (keV)", ylab = "Count")
